message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>BACKGROUND</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    A key trial (
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="clintrialgov" xlink:href="NCT04308668">NCT04308668</jats:ext-link>
                    ) of post-exposure prophylaxis found hydroxychloroquine-associated (HCQ) reductions of Covid-19 by 17% overall and 31% to 49% in subgroups. To understand these trends, we re-analyzed the dataset.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>METHODS</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Our protocol conformed to the Standard Protocol Items: Recommendations for Interventional Trials (SPIRIT). We compared the incidence of Covid-19 after HCQ or placebo, stratifying by intervention lag, age, and gender.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>RESULTS</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Newly requested data missing from the dataset revealed that 52% and 19% of subjects received medication 1-2 days after intended and assumed overnight delivery or four-day intervention lag respectively. After re-analysis, we found reduced HCQ-associated incidence of Covid-19 with Early (up to 3 days post-exposure) (RR 0.58, 95%CI 0.35 - 0.97;
                    <jats:italic>p</jats:italic>
                    =0.044; NNT 14.5) but not Late (RR 1.22, 95%CI 0.72 - 2.04) prophylaxis.
                  </jats:p>
                  <jats:p>
                    We found a significant HCQ-associated Covid-19 reduction in subjects 18 to 45 years old with Early (RR 0.54, 95%CI 0.29-0.97;
                    <jats:italic>p</jats:italic>
                    =0.0448, NNT 11.5) but not Late (RR 1.02, 95%CI 0.55-1.89) prophylaxis, attenuated in older subjects (RR 0.75, 95%CI 0-27-2.05) and by co-morbidities. Although, we did not detect effects of gender, folate, zinc, or ascorbate, confounding effects cannot be excluded.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>CONCLUSIONS</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Using novel data and prospective re-analysis, hydroxychloroquine, in an age-dependent manner, was associated with reduced Covid-19 compatible illness when supplied for post-exposure prophylaxis between 1 and 3 days after high- or moderate-risk exposure, at higher loading and maintenance doses than in similar studies. The original study conclusions are controverted, and our finding warrants prospective confirmation.</jats:p>
                  <jats:p>
                    Protocol registered at Open Science Framework:
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="http://osf.io/fqtnw">osf.io/fqtnw</jats:ext-link>
                    (last revised September 27, 2020,
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Highlights</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Missing data integrated with dataset re-analysis reversed findings of original study</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Hydroxychloroquine associated reduction (42%) of Covid-19 compatible illness found</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Effect in Post-exposure Prophylaxis when received 1-3 days after exposure</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Risk Ratio 0.58 (95% CI 0.35-0.97, p=0.044, NNT14.5)</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Findings controvert the conclusions of original study</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: Effective post-exposure prophylaxis of Covid-19 associated with hydroxychloroquine: Prospective dataset re-analysis incorporating novel, missing data
